cap_rationale_audit.interpretation: Most metrics used here are standard diagnostics, but the explicit numeric caps are local governance thresholds unless marked as nominal statistical targets. A triggered local cap should be read as WATCH/REVIEW evidence, not as proof that the model violated a universal market-standard constant.

rows.rationale: Calibration MAE is a standard reliability summary, but the 0.18 watch cap is a local tolerance chosen for dashboard governance.
rows.recommendation: Keep as WATCH until paired with relative log-loss/ECE and uncertainty-aware sample support; do not treat 0.18 as an industry constant.
rows.source_note: The threshold encodes a local risk tolerance; it is not an industry constant.

rows.rationale: Same governance tolerance as the bottom calibration cap; the metric is standard, the exact cap is local.
rows.recommendation: Prefer baseline-relative calibration diagnostics before promotion.
rows.source_note: The threshold encodes a local risk tolerance; it is not an industry constant.

rows.rationale: Log-loss and leave-one-cluster-out are standard anti-overfit tools; the meaningful test is whether the model beats raw/null baselines out of cluster.
rows.recommendation: Promote probability changes only when LOCO log-loss beats null with stable folds.
rows.source_note: The gate compares against a null, hold, simple DCA, or current live policy baseline.

rows.rationale: The 80% target follows the label of a p10-p90 interval. It is a nominal statistical target, not proof that every historical cluster must clear 80%.
rows.recommendation: Use as calibration evidence; inspect cluster failures before treating it as a blocker.
rows.source_note: The threshold is tied to a declared statistical target, such as nominal interval coverage.

rows.rationale: The target is nominal coverage; the conformal method is research-only and not a live default.
rows.recommendation: Require cluster stability before using a conformal scale in production.
rows.source_note: The threshold is tied to a declared statistical target, such as nominal interval coverage.

rows.rationale: Per-fold 80% is a deliberately strict small-sample stress gate; it is not a canonical conformal requirement.
rows.recommendation: Replace with binomial/confidence-aware fold review before any production gate.
rows.source_note: The threshold is a pre-registered stress-test heuristic and needs attainability review.

rows.metric: terminal win-rate versus median simple baseline
rows.rationale: Baseline-relative policy utility check; 60% is a local safety margin.
rows.recommendation: Keep as a dashboard WATCH gate, not as external statistical law.
rows.source_note: The gate compares against a null, hold, simple DCA, or current live policy baseline.

rows.metric: average-cost win-rate versus median simple baseline
rows.rationale: Baseline-relative entry-quality check; the exact 60% cap is local.
rows.source_note: The gate compares against a null, hold, simple DCA, or current live policy baseline.

rows.metric: worst terminal edge versus median simple baseline
rows.rationale: Left-tail guard versus simple baselines; -15% is a local drawdown tolerance.
rows.source_note: The gate compares against a null, hold, simple DCA, or current live policy baseline.

rows.rationale: Stress check over hand-picked parameter variants; 50% is an attainability floor.
rows.source_note: The threshold is a pre-registered stress-test heuristic and needs attainability review.

rows.rationale: Window Jitter anti-overfit check; 60% is local and sample-size sensitive.

rows.rationale: Distribution utility is judged versus hold; 70% is a local promotion tolerance.
rows.source_note: The gate compares against a null, hold, simple DCA, or current live policy baseline.

rows.metric: count of windows ending below 75% of hold
rows.rationale: Left-tail hardening against hold; 75% is a local capital-preservation floor.

rows.rationale: Distribution parameter perturbation stress gate; threshold is local.

rows.rationale: Date-jitter anti-overfit gate; threshold is local.

rows.metric: top-date jitter worst end value versus hold
rows.rationale: Date-jitter left-tail gate; threshold is local.

rows.metric: leave-one-top-out worst value at low versus hold
rows.rationale: Out-of-window utility must beat hold at post-top low; baseline-relative, not canonical.
rows.source_note: The gate compares against a null, hold, simple DCA, or current live policy baseline.

rows.rationale: +/-25% is a pre-registered stress band, not a canonical anti-overfit constant.
rows.recommendation: Keep as a hard common-exam blocker only with materiality review attached.
rows.source_note: The threshold is a pre-registered stress-test heuristic and needs attainability review.

rows.metric: worst terminal-ratio shift versus live policy
rows.rationale: Terminal-ratio stability cap is local; it guards sensitivity, not a standard threshold.
rows.source_note: The threshold is a pre-registered stress-test heuristic and needs attainability review.

rows.metric: held-out terminal edge deterioration versus live policy
rows.rationale: 5pp deterioration cap is a local promotion brake and currently drives delta-only review.
rows.recommendation: Before changing it, pre-register a materiality-aware replacement gate.
rows.source_note: The threshold is a pre-registered stress-test heuristic and needs attainability review.

rows.metric: inherited current-utility historical thresholds inside plateau cells
rows.rationale: These inherit current utility gates but are stricter because every plateau cell must pass.
rows.recommendation: Treat historical threshold failures as more material than delta-only heldout failures.
rows.source_note: The gate compares against a null, hold, simple DCA, or current live policy baseline.
rows.threshold_display: terminal win >=60%, cost win >=60%, worst terminal edge >=-15%

rows.rationale: 85% is a local reserve-exhaustion cap for delayed lower-low stress.
rows.source_note: The threshold is a pre-registered stress-test heuristic and needs attainability review.

rows.metric: average entry premium versus delayed final low
rows.rationale: This now enforces the pre-registered 60% average-entry-premium cap.
rows.recommendation: Treat triggered paths as common-exam blockers unless a replacement gate is pre-registered.
rows.source_note: The threshold is a pre-registered stress-test heuristic and needs attainability review.

rows.metric: terminal value versus fixed 18-week DCA on delayed lower-low path
rows.rationale: This DCA18 tolerance is enforced and now appears in the pre-registered gate text.
rows.recommendation: Keep as a baseline-relative adverse-path tolerance, not as a canonical constant.
rows.source_note: The threshold is a pre-registered stress-test heuristic and needs attainability review.

rows.metric: minimum dry reserve through first two thirds
rows.rationale: Reserve tolerance is a live-policy governance choice, not a standard constant.
rows.source_note: The threshold encodes a local risk tolerance; it is not an industry constant.

rows.metric: terminal value versus fixed 52-week DCA on false-bottom grind
rows.rationale: Requires beating a simple DCA baseline under an adverse path.
rows.source_note: The gate compares against a null, hold, simple DCA, or current live policy baseline.

rows.metric: maximum four-week deployment unlock after false-bottom bounces
rows.rationale: This now enforces the pre-registered 20pp four-week unlock cap.
rows.recommendation: Treat triggered paths as common-exam blockers unless a replacement gate is pre-registered.
rows.source_note: The threshold is a pre-registered stress-test heuristic and needs attainability review.

rows.rationale: 30% is a local participation floor for violent fast-V stress.
rows.source_note: The threshold is a pre-registered stress-test heuristic and needs attainability review.

rows.metric: terminal value versus fixed 18-week DCA on fast-V path
rows.rationale: Allows up to 10% terminal lag versus fast DCA; tolerance is local.
rows.source_note: The gate compares against a null, hold, simple DCA, or current live policy baseline.

rows.metric: terminal value versus fixed 52-week DCA on shallow recovery
rows.rationale: Allows up to 5% lag versus slow DCA when avoiding premature reserve use.
rows.source_note: The gate compares against a null, hold, simple DCA, or current live policy baseline.

rows.rationale: No early reserve exhaustion is a governance invariant for promotion.
rows.source_note: The threshold encodes a local risk tolerance; it is not an industry constant.

source_type_definitions.baseline_relative_gate: The gate compares against a null, hold, simple DCA, or current live policy baseline.
source_type_definitions.canonical_metric: The metric is standard, but any local threshold still needs separate rationale.
source_type_definitions.heuristic_stress_gate: The threshold is a pre-registered stress-test heuristic and needs attainability review.
source_type_definitions.nominal_statistical_target: The threshold is tied to a declared statistical target, such as nominal interval coverage.
source_type_definitions.policy_risk_tolerance: The threshold encodes a local risk tolerance; it is not an industry constant.

decoupled_deep_anchor_candidate.interpretation: The decoupled-deep-anchor sweep tests whether replacing the fixed forecast multiplier with asset-library support can solve the open synthetic gates without touching release mechanics. A rejected result means the remaining failure is not explained by the deep anchor alone; the evidence should be used before proposing a broader production policy change.

known_risks.evidence: The live policy now uses deploy_duration_cdf_floor() with horizon multipliers (1.0, 1.5, 2.0) instead of a single linear 52-week clock.
known_risks.risk: The updated clock must still clear the parameter plateau common exam.

known_risks.evidence: Deep anchor is coupled to the bottom forecast through DEPLOY_DEEP_ANCHOR_M=0.6; anchor-only candidate verdict=REJECTED_SYNTHETIC_GATES.
known_risks.risk: If the bottom forecast follows price lower during a delayed lower-low path, depth-floor sizing can release working capital too early; the final reserve tranche is now separately gated by non-forecast evidence.

known_risks.evidence: DEPLOY_LAST_TRANCHE_FRACTION=0.95 is locked until absolute observed drawdown exceeds the historical library or recovery/resolution confirmation fires.
known_risks.risk: Forecast error can still affect earlier reserve laddering; common-exam synthetic paths must verify the final-tranche lock is sufficient.

known_risks.evidence: Historical replay contains left-tail cost weak spots; see accumulation episode rows for negative utility or high average-cost premium cases.
known_risks.risk: The policy can win terminal value while still buying too expensively in left-tail or first-cycle regimes.

pre_registered_gates.description: Run a grid around deployment-policy parameters at 0.75x, 1.00x, and 1.25x. Include horizon, floor/release speed, deep-anchor multiplier, hard-reserve floor, catch-up speed, and late-start trigger parameters where applicable.
pass_criteria: Worst terminal-ratio shift stays within 15% of the live policy.
No grid cell fails the current historical audit thresholds.
No asset has a worse held-out terminal edge than the current live audit by more than 5 percentage points.

materiality_review.interpretation: The pre-registered plateau gate remains a FAIL when any held-out episode deteriorates by more than 5 percentage points. This review separates that delta-only failure from terminal-ratio and historical-threshold failures so future research can decide whether to harden policy parameters or pre-register a more materiality-aware gate.

result.test_design: +/-25% one-at-a-time full-stack perturbations plus the live cell

pre_registered_gates.description: Stress a path that first trades through the model anchor, bounces enough to tempt catch-up/redeploy logic, then makes a later and deeper low.
pass_criteria: Deployment at the final low remains below 85% unless an explicit capitulation confirmation fires.
Average entry premium versus the final low stays below 60%.
Terminal value stays within 10% of fixed 18-week DCA.

pre_registered_gates.description: Stress a false-bottom path with repeated shallow rallies and lower lows over a longer-than-expected horizon.
pass_criteria: Policy preserves at least the hard reserve floor until the final third of the path.
Terminal value beats fixed 52-week DCA after fees/slippage assumptions used by the research engine.
No single false-bottom bounce unlocks more than 20 percentage points of additional deployment.

pre_registered_gates.description: Stress a violent fast-V liquidation where a too-slow policy misses the low and pays for excess reserve.
pass_criteria: Policy deploys at least 30% by the fast-V low.
Terminal value stays within 10% of fixed 18-week DCA.

pre_registered_gates.description: Stress a shallow correction that never reaches the deep anchor and then recovers.
pass_criteria: Terminal value stays within 5% of fixed 52-week DCA.
The shallow governor prevents a non-violent correction from exhausting reserve early.

pre_registered_gates.description: Detect paths where reserve is exhausted before the realized or synthetic low and report them as hard governance failures unless explicitly waived in pre-registration.
pass_criteria: No historical episode triggers early exhaustion.
No synthetic adverse path triggers early exhaustion.
Any policy candidate that triggers early exhaustion is blocked from dashboard promotion.

promotion_rule: A future policy may be labelled fully promoted only if both the current historical utility audit and the common-exam gates pass. Until then, dashboard PASS labels must be scoped as current utility audit only.

re_entry_candidates.description: Test an anchor partly tied to realized valuation support or prior-cycle support rather than only the current bottom forecast multiplier.
Keep a larger late reserve until capitulation, time exhaustion, or recovery above a predeclared markup confirms.
Limit catch-up and redeploy releases after shallow bounces unless final-low risk has materially decayed.

relationship_to_current_verdict.reason: The existing PASS covers utility, historical replay, jitter, target 90/110/lagged perturbations, leave-one-episode-out, and chronological holdout. The common exam below is a stricter promotion layer and should be read separately from the current utility verdict and from the Fable 2026-07-05 core-only cross-audit.

release_hardening_candidate.interpretation: The release-hardening overlay clears the delayed-lower-low average entry premium and false-bottom four-week unlock synthetic gates by throttling early front-loading and holding a late reserve until a non-forecast trigger fires. On the real accumulation episodes the same deferral buys later and higher in prolonged deep bears (2018 BTC/ETH worst), so terminal value and average cost both deteriorate versus live. Under the pre-registered promotion rule (historical utility AND common exam) the candidate is not promotable; it is recorded as a REJECTED research candidate with an explicit trade-off, not a production change.

summary: The current dashboard verdict is scoped to the historical utility audit. The stricter common-exam gates now replay parameter plateau and synthetic adverse paths through the dashboard full stack. Fable's external core-only cross-audit remains recorded as a separate warning because it failed the convex core on plateau and synthetic-shape stress.
